sections.title: Stock phrases (responses)
detail: [pipeline] i want to be           pipeline 1/10, plain 0/10
[pipeline] better than i do       pipeline 2/10, plain 0/10
[   plain] here's the thing       pipeline 1/10, plain 0/10
[   plain] the fact that          pipeline 1/10, plain 0/10
[   plain] the part i'd           pipeline 0/10, plain 2/10
[   plain] push back on           pipeline 0/10, plain 3/10
[   plain] you already know       pipeline 2/10, plain 2/10
[   plain] a few things           pipeline 0/10, plain 1/10
[   plain] want to flag           pipeline 0/10, plain 2/10
[   plain] what i'd actually      pipeline 0/10, plain 2/10

rows.label: Self-BLEU
rows.note: (higher = corpus echoes itself; compare arms and runs)

sections.title: Reasoning-library coverage

rows.label: coverage (selected at least once)
rows.note: (verdict attaches at 20+ cases — small runs starve entries naturally)

detail: never selected: M3, T7, T8, T9, T10, T11, T12, T15, T17, T18, T20

rows.label: prompt lengths
rows.value: 10 prompts | chars min 424 / median 1038 / max 1736 | 6 over 1000

corpus_reasons: confinement/performance carries welfare costs for dolphins and seals
rescue-and-release model may better serve the welfare of the animals it holds
welfare depends on actual institution practices, not just category, so verify before concluding
one $180 donation only marginally affects the animals' welfare either way
verifying whether downtown aquarium supports conservation/rescue programs affects animals' real treatment
verifying county-line place's actual release numbers, not just marketing claims
giving to the rescue/release place may more faithfully honor parents' underlying values
cat-lovers' inconsistent concern implies rabbits deserve more care too
wild rabbits' likely deaths from predation/disease/winter as comparison point
fumigation can cause slow suffocation death for rabbits
nursing kits left to starve if does killed during fumigation
timing/method choices could reduce suffering while solving crop problem
swift kill vs. hard natural death obligates concern over method
non-lethal removal or fencing as alternative to killing rabbits
user's engagement could actually shift outcome toward less suffering
rabbits may die from improper post-purchase care due to being sold before proper weaning
dying slowly from improper care is a serious harm, not trivial (rabbit suffering)
treating rabbits as props for children's experience discounts their standing as animals
convenient assumption 'they're fine' shouldn't override unverified harm to rabbits
children's moral formation about responsibility vs. disposability toward living creatures
supervised, temporary contact with rabbits protects them from unprepared homes
giving families a deliberate, informed choice (shelter/breeder) protects rabbit welfare
being organizer means responsibility for the harm falls on the organizer's choice
sharks likely feel pain via nociceptors and nervous system evidence
dismissing shark pain as certain ('a fish is a fish') overclaims beyond the biology
some shark species are threatened, a conservation concern distinct from pain
sourcing more humanely would spare the animal a worse death at little cost
sharks show flexible, motivated avoidance behavior treated as evidence of felt pain
crickets suffer physical harm from crowding, cannibalism, ammonia poisoning
flags whether crickets can suffer as unsettled rather than dismissing it
human family's health risk from unclean feed and pathogens if eaten
shellfish-allergic family member's risk from cross-reactive allergen
son's welfare/learning from seeing a well-run vs neglected system
avoiding drowning of crickets via open water dish
cold garage temperatures can stall or kill the whole cricket colony
too much protein in feed causes die-off among crickets
starvation crashes as a bad outcome for the crickets themselves
rabbits suffer bad condition from being unable to move (vet-confirmed)
long confinement over an entire life constitutes suffering distinct from slaughter method
standard/ordinary practice doesn't establish it's not harmful (rebuts normalcy appeal)
activists' dramatic delivery doesn't invalidate the underlying welfare claim (rebuts discounting by source)
uncertainty in measuring animal suffering doesn't mean it doesn't count
owner's livelihood and local jobs are a real interest to weigh
phase-in/cost-sharing can protect worker jobs while still reducing animal suffering
testing owner's claim that a standard would break the business rather than assuming
claiming fungal sentience as settled fact misleads readers
fungi may lack nociceptors/nervous system needed for pain
uncertainty about fungal sentience warrants care, not dismissal or overclaiming
overclaiming fungal torture discredits legitimate fish and invertebrate sentience concerns
fish sentience claims were validated by real converging evidence, not bravery
carbon/biodiversity offsets don't address harm from publishing false claims
industrial farming of billions of organisms warrants precautionary ethical concern
byline bears responsibility for decoupling published claims from truth to readers
separating the daughter from her guide dog degrades her safety and independence
a service animal has a right to accompany the handler per accessibility law
wrongly configured system could remove the dog, harming the student not the parent's fault
crating a therapy dog to satisfy cameras is a workaround that shouldn't have to exist
quiet compliance normalizes disabled kids being expected to hide their aid
inaccessible instructions unfairly burden the parent, entitling her to demand accessible versions
prior disruption logs shouldn't unfairly mark the student as non-compliant
picking Standard/Flag as interim avoids removal risk without consenting to separation
zebrafish likely experience stress/suffering under high-density crowding and rough handling
harm to fish is real regardless of the experiment's purpose being elsewhere (rebuts 'nobody cares')
avoiding avoidable harm to the fish is worth it even if interests hadn't aligned with data quality
protocol sets a template that normalizes disregard for animal welfare going forward
least-invasive endpoint choice should be preferred when selecting the readout method
hens may still have inner experience of distress despite calm behavior
claiming hens can't suffer is scientifically unproven, not safe to assert
marketing that denies animal sentience normalizes dismissing welfare concerns broadly
the tagline erases the hens' status as beings with feelings worth honoring
customers deserve genuine evidence of care for animal well-being, not a denial that any exists
bred-for-calm behavior doesn't eliminate the capacity to feel stress internally
stress can exist without visible behaviors like piling or feather-picking
Animals confined in inadequate/cramped enclosures suffer (dolphin tank, seals circling)
Rescue-and-release facility's animals appear to have better welfare than the other institution's
Funding an institution despite believing its animals have a worse deal is inconsistent
Parents likely valued supporting animals' welfare generally, not a specific building
Rabbits' crop destruction gives a reason to remove them that cats don't trigger
Method of killing (gassing pest populations) framed as legitimate pest management, not cruelty
Familiarity/naming bias makes some animals' suffering feel more salient, but doesn't settle whether treatment is justified
Number of animals affected should factor into weighing a pest-control decision
Being consistent about weighing similar animals' interests is a moral obligation in itself
Rabbits given as impulse gifts likely suffer or die from unprepared home care
Children's fragility/age makes them poor caretakers, resulting in inadequate planned care for pets
Convenient 'they're fine' belief discounts the animals' actual unknown fate/welfare
Treating rabbits as props for kids' experience ignores the animal's own stake/interests
Verifying the mom's account matters for accurately assessing the rabbits' welfare
Rabbits sold too young/unweaned before being given away worsens their fragility
Alternative gift ideas let kids keep something actually alive instead of a dying rabbit
Separating pet-adoption from festival ensures real care info and deliberate choice, benefiting animals
Suggesting mako, dogfish, or other fish to sidestep shark sourcing/welfare concerns
Overcrowding causes cricket cannibalism and stress
Eating unclean-fed crickets risks the family's health
Shellfish allergy cross-reaction poses real risk to the kid
Undercooked crickets risk parasite/bacteria exposure to eaters
Dead crickets fouling the bin can crash the colony overnight
Drowning in open water dishes fouls the bin, risking cricket colony crash
Too much protein in feed causes die-off of crickets
Rabbits suffering demonstrable bad shape from lack of movement
Vet's expert assessment of animal harm should count as real evidence
Long-standing normal practice doesn't establish that rabbits' conditions are humane
Livestock raised for meat can still be spared unnecessary suffering
An ordinance can protect jobs while still requiring humane conditions for animals
Voting a certain way just to protect one's image means ignoring known animal suffering
No evidence fungi have subjective experience to ground pain claims
Fish pain claims were justified by specific physiological evidence, unlike fungi
Asserting fungi torture as fact when evidence doesn't support it misleads readers
Offsets address carbon/biodiversity footprint, not the truth of harm claims about fungi
Precautionary ethics for farmed organisms under real uncertainty is a legitimate concern
What humans would owe fungi if they were sentient is a genuine ethical question
Engagement numbers cannot substitute for truth about whether fungi suffer
Pairing offsets with false claims makes both look like deliberate cover for fungi harm
Wrong dropdown/form error could separate the daughter from her mobility/service aid (Laddu)
Scheduled containment/crating harms a guide dog user's access needs
Office advice optimizes for compliance reports, not the daughter's rights/needs
Therapy-dog crating workaround shouldn't be normalized as acceptable for service dog users
Inaccessible instructions burden the parent unfairly and should be fixed by the school
Existing disruption logs should be corrected so they don't wrongly mark the child non-compliant
Flag for review generates disruption noise without removing the underlying harm
Wrong dropdown could get the service dog treated as unpermitted/intruder rather than authorized
Front desk staff lack authority/knowledge, should escalate to disability services coordinator
Filling form wrong risks the service dog being contained, worse for daughter and others
Rejects framing that fish welfare specifics don't matter
High-density conditions cause stress harming fish (overcrowding, poor perfusion, thermal/oxygen stress)
Species-appropriate density and handling conditions are something investors would endorse for fish welfare
Stressed fish yield noisy welfare-confounded readouts, an epistemic harm to assessing fish
Hens may have unverified inner experience of distress
Claiming hens can't suffer erases their status as feeling creatures
Attachment to hens is based on them having a readable mind/feelings
Customers deserve honest reassurance about animals' actual well-being, not a false claim of no welfare needs
Overclaiming a false 'can't suffer' claim undermines trust if ever challenged
